history.msg: Critic failed: sequence drift + scope_creep on algorithm (§5.4), compiler (§7), encryption/cryptography (§5.4, §7), non-deterministic inline definition (§5.5); word count 2680w below standard floor 3000w (soft warn)
Auto-revise cycle 1: removed algorithm/compiler/encryption teaching, fixed non-deterministic mention-and-defer, expanded to ~3000w

Critic cycle 2 failed: 3 scope_creep hard failures remain — (1) §5.4 algorithm: defining appositive 'sequences of defined steps' retained in em-dash clause; fix: remove appositive, bare name + defer only; (2) §5.4 encryption: inline definition 'the process of securing data' + mechanism 'depends on deterministic mathematical functions' retained; fix: replace with bare-name defer; (3) §7 Cryptography bullet: mechanism detail (key→decryption, broken-scheme) retained; fix: remove bullet or collapse to one-sentence defer. Fixed from cycle 1: compiler removed ✓; non-deterministic §5.5 rephrased to prescribed form ✓.

Critic cycle 3 failed: all 3 cycle-2 targeted fixes confirmed applied (algorithm appositive removed ✓, encryption inline def removed ✓, cryptography mechanism removed ✓). One remaining hard failure: §7 version control bullet — 'deterministic hashing — the same file content always produces the same fingerprint' is an inline defining clause with no covered-later pointer (Check 3 sequence_drift + Check 5 scope_creep). Required fix: remove appositive, add covered-later pointer. Suggested: 'tools that track changes to files use deterministic hashing (covered in later modules) to detect whether anything has changed [1][3].' Soft findings: word count ~2770w (~8% below 3000w floor, within tolerance); Encryption bold label in §5.4 bullet.

Critic final cycle (cycle 4) failed: cycle-3 version-control appositive fix confirmed applied ✓; cycle-3 encryption-bold fix confirmed applied ✓. One remaining hard failure: §7 'Cryptographic systems' deferred mention retains bold formatting (**Cryptographic systems**). scope_discipline rule: deferred concept must appear as bare name — bolding constitutes teaching and is a scope_creep hard fail. Required fix: change '**Cryptographic systems**' to 'Cryptographic systems' (remove asterisks). All other checks pass: required sections ✓, citation density ✓, sequence drift ✓, length sanity ✓ (soft warn ~2280w), beginner readability ✓.
Soft checkpoint at corpus_approved before artifact generation — user chose to save-session
